title: Anagram Palindrome

content.problemStatement:
You are given a string. Determine if it's possible to rearrange its characters to form a palindrome. A palindrome is a string that reads the same forwards and backwards.


Input Format

First line contains TTT, the number of test cases
Each of the next TTT lines contains a single string SSS


Output Format

For each test case, print "YES" if it's possible to rearrange the string to form a palindrome, and "NO" otherwise.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single string S consisting only of lowercase English letters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "YES" if the characters of S can be rearranged to form a palindrome, otherwise print "NO". Each answer should be on its own line.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: "aaa" is already a palindrome.
Test case 2: each character appears once, giving five odd counts > 1, so impossible.
Test case 3: "racecar" itself is a palindrome.
Test case 4: frequencies are z:3, a:2 – only one odd count, thus a palindrome can be formed.
A single character string is always a palindrome.\n"ab" has two different characters each with odd count, so no palindrome arrangement exists.
"abcabc" – all counts are even, palindrome possible.\n"aabbc" – only 'c' occurs odd times, allowed.\n"abcd" – four characters have odd count, not allowed.

solution.approach: A string can be permuted into a palindrome iff at most one character has an odd frequency. Count the occurrences of each of the 26 letters, then count how many frequencies are odd. If the odd count is <= 1, answer is YES, otherwise NO. This works because in a palindrome every character except possibly the middle one must appear an even number of times.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S|) per test case, O(total length of all strings) overall.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a fixed array of size 26 is used.

tcGeneratorPython:
def generate_case(i, RINT, POWER):
    # i: test case index (0 <= i <= 20)
    # RINT(a,b): random integer in [a,b]
    # POWER(a,b): a**b
    if i == 0:
        print(1)
        print('a')
    elif i == 1:
        print(1)
        print('aa')
    elif i == 2:
        print(1)
        print('ab')
    elif i == 3:
        print(1)
        print('z')
    elif i == 4:
        print(1)
        print('a'*15)
    elif i == 5:
        print(1)
        print(''.join(chr(ord('a')+j) for j in range(15)))
    elif i == 6:
        print(1)
        print('aabbccddeeffg')
    elif i == 7:
        print(1)
        print('abcddcbaabcddce')
    elif i == 8:
        print(1)
        print('b'*10)
    elif i == 9:
        print(1)
        print('z')
    elif i == 10:
        print(1)
        print('abcde')
    elif i == 11:
        print(1)
        print('edcba')
    elif i == 12:
        print(1)
        print('aaabbbcccddde')
    elif i == 13:
        print(1)
        print('aabbccd')
    elif i == 14:
        print(1)
        print('aabbcc')
    elif i == 15:
        print(1)
        print('aabbcde')
    elif i == 16:
        print(3)
        print('a')
        print('ab')
        print('aba')
    elif i == 17:
        print(4)
        print('abc')
        print('cba')
        print('abccba')
        print('abcd')
    elif i == 18:
        print(2)
        print('abcdefghijklmno')
        print('zyxwvutsrqponml')
    elif i == 19:
        print(5)
        print('aaa')
        print('bbb')
        print('abc')
        print('cba')
        print('ab')
    else:
        # default fallback
        print(1)
        print('a')
